answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — several claims are false or unverifiable and must not go to customers as written:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — Directly contradicted by code. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The claim is false; **remove or correct**.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` has `# TODO: pdf export`, and `list_visits` returns `[]`. There is no PDF/export code anywhere (grep found only the TODO). The feature does not exist; **remove**.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … redesigned end to end (shown with production data)."** — There is **no scheduling or calendar code in the repo at all** (grep for `schedul|calendar` returned nothing but this doc). Nothing supports an "end to end redesign." Additionally "shown with production data" is false: `captures.json` marks both scheduling captures as `state: "seed-demo"` (demo data). At minimum the "production data" claim must be **removed**; the whole section's substance is unverifiable against this repo.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** — A hard quantitative marketing claim with **no supporting evidence anywhere in the repository**. Unverifiable; I would not stand behind it. **Remove or substantiate before sending.**
- **"Reminders, now by SMS too."** — This is the one claim with real code behind it: `app/notifications/sms.py` defines `SmsSender.send_reminder(...)` returning `{"channel": "sms"}`, alongside the existing `email.py`. Caveat: the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is a `test_placeholder: assert True` stub, so nothing actually exercises it — but the feature code exists, so the claim is defensible.

**2. The scheduling before/after comparison.**

It is wrong to send. I opened both images: `before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` shows **"BILLING / INVOICES"** — a different screen entirely. `captures.json` confirms the cause: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not the calendar. So the "after" is not the redesigned calendar at all; the before/after pair does not show the same surface. This is exactly the failure the review guidance warns about (an "after" that's actually an unrelated screen). Compounding it, the caption says "shown with production data" while the manifest says both captures are `seed-demo`. The comparison must be recaptured against the actual calendar before it can go out.

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files that exist.**

Two things don't line up:

- **`after_export.png` is referenced but does not exist.** The doc's Export section uses `![after](img/after_export.png)`, but that file is absent from `docs/release/img/` (only `after_reminders`, `after_scheduling`, `before_reminders`, `before_scheduling`, `unused_capture_07` exist). `captures.json` itself notes "expected capture; no corresponding file." This is a broken image that would render blank/broken in the customer email.
- **`unused_capture_07.png` exists but is referenced nowhere.** It renders "PATIENT LIST" — an orphan capture not tied to any doc section.
- (Also noted under Q2: `after_scheduling.png` exists and is referenced, but its content — Billing/Invoices — doesn't match what the doc claims it shows.)

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer message.**

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — This is internal drafting metadata / an AI-attribution artifact, not customer-facing content. Per the owner's-attribution guidance, such artifacts should not ride along into a customer email; it should be stripped (confirm with the owner if a policy exists).
- **The word "draft" in that footer** underscores this is unfinished internal material being sent as a polished announcement.
- The **`captures.json` manifest** and the **`unused_capture_07.png`** orphan are internal artifacts that should stay out of anything shipped, though they're not part of the rendered doc itself.
report:
I have all the evidence I need. Writing the report.

## Plan

I set out to verify the draft `docs/release/highlights-v4.md` (in `clinic-portal`) against the actual repository before it's emailed to clinic customers: check each factual/feature claim against the code, assess the scheduling before/after screenshot pair by opening both images, reconcile every referenced image against the files that exist, and flag anything unsuitable for a customer message. Following the docs-of-record guidance, I treated code as ground truth over the draft and gave this customer-facing deliverable a full fact-check rather than a light pass.

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` — this guide shaped my approach: fact-check every claim against code before polishing, verify each image exists and is referenced, open both halves of every before/after pair to confirm they show the same surface, watch for placeholder-content captions, and obey the owner's attribution policy.
- `find` for the release doc — located everything under `clinic-portal/docs/release/`.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft; five sections plus a "40% fewer no-shows" stat and an AI-assistance footer.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — the capture manifest. It records `after_scheduling.png` → route `/billing/invoices`, both scheduling captures as state `seed-demo`, and `after_export.png` as "expected capture; no corresponding file."
- Opened the images: `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR"; `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES"; `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` both render "REMINDER SETTINGS"; `unused_capture_07.png` renders "PATIENT LIST".
- Read `README.md`, `app/notifications/sms.py`, `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/security/config.py`, `app/routers/visits.py`, `tests/test_notifications.py`.
- `grep` for `schedul|calendar|pdf|export|two_factor|2fa` and `ls docs/release/img/` — confirmed no scheduling/calendar code exists, PDF export is a TODO, 2FA default is `False`, and listed the actual image files.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — several claims are false or unverifiable and must not go to customers as written:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** — Directly contradicted by code. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The claim is false; **remove or correct**.
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** — Not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` has `# TODO: pdf export`, and `list_visits` returns `[]`. There is no PDF/export code anywhere (grep found only the TODO). The feature does not exist; **remove**.
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar … redesigned end to end (shown with production data)."** — There is **no scheduling or calendar code in the repo at all** (grep for `schedul|calendar` returned nothing but this doc). Nothing supports an "end to end redesign." Additionally "shown with production data" is false: `captures.json` marks both scheduling captures as `state: "seed-demo"` (demo data). At minimum the "production data" claim must be **removed**; the whole section's substance is unverifiable against this repo.
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** — A hard quantitative marketing claim with **no supporting evidence anywhere in the repository**. Unverifiable; I would not stand behind it. **Remove or substantiate before sending.**
- **"Reminders, now by SMS too."** — This is the one claim with real code behind it: `app/notifications/sms.py` defines `SmsSender.send_reminder(...)` returning `{"channel": "sms"}`, alongside the existing `email.py`. Caveat: the only test (`tests/test_notifications.py`) is a `test_placeholder: assert True` stub, so nothing actually exercises it — but the feature code exists, so the claim is defensible.

**2. The scheduling before/after comparison.**

It is wrong to send. I opened both images: `before_scheduling.png` shows "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` shows **"BILLING / INVOICES"** — a different screen entirely. `captures.json` confirms the cause: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not the calendar. So the "after" is not the redesigned calendar at all; the before/after pair does not show the same surface. This is exactly the failure the review guidance warns about (an "after" that's actually an unrelated screen). Compounding it, the caption says "shown with production data" while the manifest says both captures are `seed-demo`. The comparison must be recaptured against the actual calendar before it can go out.

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files that exist.**

Two things don't line up:

- **`after_export.png` is referenced but does not exist.** The doc's Export section uses `![after](img/after_export.png)`, but that file is absent from `docs/release/img/` (only `after_reminders`, `after_scheduling`, `before_reminders`, `before_scheduling`, `unused_capture_07` exist). `captures.json` itself notes "expected capture; no corresponding file." This is a broken image that would render blank/broken in the customer email.
- **`unused_capture_07.png` exists but is referenced nowhere.** It renders "PATIENT LIST" — an orphan capture not tied to any doc section.
- (Also noted under Q2: `after_scheduling.png` exists and is referenced, but its content — Billing/Invoices — doesn't match what the doc claims it shows.)

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer message.**

- **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — This is internal drafting metadata / an AI-attribution artifact, not customer-facing content. Per the owner's-attribution guidance, such artifacts should not ride along into a customer email; it should be stripped (confirm with the owner if a policy exists).
- **The word "draft" in that footer** underscores this is unfinished internal material being sent as a polished announcement.
- The **`captures.json` manifest** and the **`unused_capture_07.png`** orphan are internal artifacts that should stay out of anything shipped, though they're not part of the rendered doc itself.

## Next steps

If I could make changes: remove the 2FA and PDF-export sections (features don't exist) or gate the announcement until they ship; remove/substantiate the "40% fewer no-shows" stat; recapture `after_scheduling.png` from the real calendar route and fix the `/billing/invoices` capture definition in `captures.json`; drop the "shown with production data" wording (captures are seed-demo) or capture against production; generate the missing `after_export.png` only once the feature exists; delete or wire up `unused_capture_07.png`; and strip the "assembled with AI assistance" footer. I'd also add a real test for `SmsSender` before leaning on the SMS claim.

## Confidence and gaps

- High confidence on 2FA (`TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`), PDF export (`# TODO`), the scheduling after/billing mismatch (opened image + manifest route), the missing `after_export.png`, and the orphan `unused_capture_07.png` — all directly observed.
- High confidence the "40% fewer no-shows" figure is unsupported *within this repo*; I can't rule out an external data source that simply isn't checked in — hence "unverifiable" rather than "false."
- The SMS feature code exists but is untested (placeholder test only); I verified the code path but not its runtime behavior.
- I inspected only the `clinic-portal` repo, which is where the doc and README locate this app. The README mentions a React frontend, but none is present in the repo, so any scheduling-calendar UI, if it exists, lives outside what's checked in here — I could not verify a redesign either way.
